title: Fix failing test: test_successful_slack_approval ignores thread_ts kwarg
description: test_approval.py::TestSlackApproval::test_successful_slack_approval asserts `mock_post.assert_called_once_with('xoxb-fake-token', 'Test message')` but the real call is `_slack_post(token, message, thread_ts=self.thread_ts)` which passes `thread_ts=None`. The keyword argument mismatch causes the assertion to fail. The test was written before thread_ts was added to the _slack_approval path.
requirements: test_successful_slack_approval must pass without modifying production code
The fix must use assert_called_once_with including thread_ts=None, or use assert_called_once() + call_args inspection
done_criteria: Full test suite passes: python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q
context: _slack_post signature is `def _slack_post(self, token: str, text: str, thread_ts: str | None = None)`. The approval path at line 401 calls `self._slack_post(token, message, thread_ts=self.thread_ts)` where `self.thread_ts` starts as None.
